briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: init method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: eq method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: predecessor method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: successor method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: str method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: repr method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: is_valid method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: LinePrefix for document structure extraction pipeline

detaileddescription.para: Class for line prefix typical for lists items. For example for line
1) Bla bla bla prefix is Prefix( 1) )
For line a) some item with letter prefix is Prefix( a) )
For line 1.2.1 Dotted line prefix is Prefix( 1.2.1 )
Can be used to define if one prefix is valid predecessor for this prefix.
